articles.title: 2 Defence stocks to watch as India pivots to proprietary nuclear and maritime tech
articles.description: India’s defence indigenisation push is creating a unique opportunity for nimble small-cap players to build "Bluechip-like" competitive moats. Two such underdog companies that have pivoted from industrial components to mission-critical defence systems, focusing on proprietary technology rather than just assembly, have caught the fancy of many investors.
articles.content:
The Indigenisation Super-Cycle: Why India’s ‘Silent’ Suppliers are Moving Up the Value Chain

articles.title: Influencer Marketing Technology: Measuring the $25 Billion Market
articles.description: Influencer marketing has traversed a remarkable arc from experimental social media tactic to a $25 billion global industry that sits at the intersection of media, data science, and creator economics. What began as brands sending free products to bloggers with large followings has evolved into a sophisticated technology-driven ecosystem with dedicated platforms, performance measurement frameworks, […]

articles.title: Dr Jitendra reviews Island projects with experts, Ocean technologists
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent KAVARATTI, LAKSHADWEEP, Mar 7 : Union Minister for Earth Sciences and Science and Technology Dr. Jitendra Singh reviewed the progress of key ocean technology projects being implemented by the National Institute of Ocean Technology (NIOT) in Lakshadweep, with a focus on strengthening water security and advancing sustainable energy solutions for the island territory. During the review meeting with NIOT scientists and officials in Kavaratti, the Minister assessed the functioning of the existing Low Temperature Thermal Desalination (LTTD) plants […]

Excelsior Correspondent

articles.description: Hyderabad: Telangana's technology sector generates over $40 billion (approx Rs 3.6 lakh crore) in IT exports, even as the nearly 400 global capability.
articles.content: Hyderabad: Telangana's technology sector generates over $40 billion (approx Rs 3.6 lakh crore) in IT exports, even as the nearly 400 global capability centres (GCCs) currently housed in Hyderabad employ over three lakh professionals, IT and industrie... [1600 chars]

articles.title: Dr Jitendra interacts with beneficiaries of Lakshadweep Desalination Plant, exciting experiences narrated
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent KAVARATTI, LAKSHADWEEP, Mar 7 :  Earlier we used to drink salty well water. Now everyone in our area is using desalinated water for drinking,” said Abdul Rehman, a resident of Kavaratti, while interacting with Union Minister for Earth Sciences and Science and Technology Dr. Jitendra Singh during his visit to the Low Temperature Thermal Desalination (LTTD) plant in the island on Friday. Rehman was among several local residents who shared their experiences with the Minister as he reviewed the […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Correspondent
KAVARATTI, LAKSHADWEEP, Mar 7 : Earlier we used to drink salty well water. Now everyone in our area is using desalinated water for drinking,” said Abdul Rehman, a resident of Kavaratti, while interacting with Union Minister fo... [2156 chars]

articles.title: Editorial | Smooth roll-out of surveillance tech will help Hong Kong combat crime
articles.description: While cameras and facial recognition technology must be deployed carefully, their use can ultimately help ensure public safety.
articles.content: Facial recognition technology, which can match faces in public places to databases, helping police identify suspects, is set to be introduced this year. It is taking longer than expected to roll out. The police are still resolving legal and technical... [1523 chars]

articles.description: Chennai: Researchers at Indian Institute of Technology Madras have developed a compact high-voltage electronic system that could help small satellites.

articles.title: Commentary: Hormuz is the hidden risk to the AI economy
articles.description: The building blocks of the technology industry are deeply dependent on petroleum flowing through the Strait of Hormuz, says David Fickling for Bloomberg Opinion.
articles.content:
SYDNEY: A world where we can cook up AI videos in seconds from the apps on our phones might seem remote from the physical realities of warfare in the seaways of the Persian Gulf. In fact, they’re closely intertwined.
That’s because the building block... [5103 chars]
